title: IndexProjectGraph
description: Hosts (`graph index`, SDK `runIndexProjectGraph`) currently assemble workspace targets, merge graph configuration, and invoke `provider.index()` inline. `IndexProjectGraph` extracts that orchestration so adapters handle only I/O formatting, locks, and subprocess isolation while the code-graph package owns index execution semantics.
context: Hosts (`graph index`, SDK `runIndexProjectGraph`) currently assemble workspace targets, merge graph configuration, and invoke `provider.index()` inline. `IndexProjectGraph` extracts that orchestration so adapters handle only I/O formatting, locks, and subprocess isolation while the code-graph package owns index execution semantics.

rules:
`IndexProjectGraph.execute(input)` MUST call `provider.index()` with an `IndexOptions` object built from:

- `projectRoot`
- `workspaces`
- `graphConfig`
- `codeGraphVersion`
- optional `vcsRef`
- optional `onProgress`

and MUST return the resulting `IndexResult` unchanged.
When `input.force` is `true`, `IndexProjectGraph` MUST call `provider.recreate()` before `provider.index()`. When `force` is `false` or omitted, it MUST NOT recreate the store.

rules.requirement: Accepts open provider and prepared inputs
rules:
`IndexProjectGraphInput` MUST include:

- `provider: CodeGraphProvider` (already opened)
- `projectRoot: string`
- `workspaces: WorkspaceIndexTarget[]`
- `graphConfig: ProjectGraphConfig`
- `codeGraphVersion: string`
- optional `vcsRef: string`
- optional `force: boolean`
- optional `onProgress: IndexProgressCallback`

The use case MUST NOT resolve workspaces from `specd.yaml`, acquire locks, or spawn worker processes — callers supply prepared targets and config.
`createIndexProjectGraph()` in composition MUST return a stateless `IndexProjectGraph` instance.

constraints: MUST NOT open or close the provider.
MUST NOT acquire or release the graph indexing lock.
Per-file index errors are reported in IndexResult.errors; the use case MUST NOT throw for parse failures.

scenarios.name: Incremental index
given: an opened provider and prepared IndexProjectGraphInput
then: provider.index() is called once with merged options
AND the returned IndexResult is passed through unchanged

scenarios.name: Progress callback forwarded
then: the callback is passed to provider.index()

scenarios.requirement: Accepts open provider and prepared inputs
scenarios.name: Does not resolve workspaces or acquire lock
then: it does not call lock helpers or read specd.yaml

scenarios.name: Factory returns stateless instance
then: it returns an IndexProjectGraph with no captured config

optimizedDescription: Orchestrates graph indexing with prepared inputs, optional force recreate, and no host-side lock or provider lifecycle work.
optimizedContext:
# IndexProjectGraph
## Rules
- `IndexProjectGraph.execute(input)` MUST call `provider.index()` with `projectRoot`, `workspaces`, `graphConfig`, `codeGraphVersion`, optional `vcsRef`, optional `onProgress`; return `IndexResult` unchanged.
- If `input.force` is `true`, MUST call `provider.recreate()` before `provider.index()`; otherwise MUST NOT recreate store.
- `IndexProjectGraphInput` MUST include opened `provider`, `projectRoot`, `workspaces`, `graphConfig`, `codeGraphVersion`, optional `vcsRef`, optional `force`, optional `onProgress`.
- `createIndexProjectGraph()` MUST return stateless `IndexProjectGraph`.
## Constraints
- MUST NOT open or close provider.
- MUST NOT acquire or release graph indexing lock.
- MUST NOT resolve workspaces from `specd.yaml` or spawn worker processes; callers supply prepared targets and config.
- Per-file parse failures stay in `IndexResult.errors`; use case MUST NOT throw for them.
